protocol.name: xfwl4_compositor_ui_private_v1

description.summary: Private protocol for xfwl4 compositor UI communication

protocol.description: This protocol is used internally by the xfwl4 compositor to communicate with its UI helper process. It is not intended for use by any other clients (and is not exposed to any other clients) and may change without notice.

interface.name: xfwl4_ui_manager_v1
interface.description: The compositor advertises this global only to its own UI helper process. It provides events for creating UI sub-objects (tabwin, window menu) and handles cross-cutting concerns like theme colors.

request.name: theme_color
request.description: Sent when the GTK theme changes. One request per color.

request.name: theme_colors_done

description.summary: All theme colors have been sent

request.description: Marks the end of a batch of theme_color requests.

event.name: create_tabwin
event.description: Creates a new tabwin object, which the compositor populates in two phases (see xfwl4_ui_tabwin_v1). In phase one it sends the tabwin configuration and one window event per toplevel carrying static properties only (no preview or icon), each followed by a window done event, then a tabwin done event. The UI process then reports its preview and icon sizes via xfwl4_ui_tabwin_v1.image_sizes, after which the compositor sends preview and icon data for each window in phase two.

event.name: create_window_menu
event.description: Creates a new window menu object. The compositor will send state events, workspace events, and adjacent_monitor events on the menu object, followed by a done event, after which the UI process should create the menu and send a ready request.

event.name: quit
event.description: The UI process should clean up and exit.

interface.name: xfwl4_ui_tabwin_v1
interface.description: Represents an active tab switching session. Created by the compositor via xfwl4_ui_manager_v1.create_tabwin. The compositor sends client and lifecycle events; the UI process sends interaction requests.

request.name: hover

description.summary: A window was hovered in the tabwin

request.description: The user hovered over a window entry in the tab switcher.

request.name: image_sizes

description.summary: Report the preview and icon sizes the tabwin wants

request.description:
Sent by the UI process once it has received the phase-one done event and computed its layout. Reports the sizes, in logical pixels, at which the tabwin will display window previews and application icons.
If window previews are disabled, the UI process may send 0.
On receiving this request the compositor renders each window's preview and chooses its icon at the requested size (and selected output's scale), then sends the preview and app_icon events and a second done event for each window, followed by a second tabwin done event (phase two).

arg.name: preview_size
arg.summary: Preview edge length, in logical pixels

arg.name: icon_size
arg.summary: Application icon edge length, in logical pixels

request.name: finished
request.description: The user selected a window. This destroys the tabwin object.

request.name: dismissed
request.description: The user cancelled tab switching without selecting a window. This destroys the tabwin object.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Tells the compositor that the UI process is destroying the tabwin object.

event.name: output

description.summary: The output the tabwin is shown on

event.description: Sent in phase one, before the first done. Gives the dimensions, in logical pixels, of the output the tab switcher will be displayed on (the full output, including any area covered by panels), and that output's integer scale factor. The UI process uses these to lay out the switcher and to compute the preview and icon sizes it reports with image_sizes.

event.name: mode
event.description: Sent before done.

event.name: show_window_previews

description.summary: Whether to show window previews, or just icons

event.description: Sent before done.

event.name: window_opacity
event.description: Sent before done.

event.name: initial_selection
event.description: Sent before done.

event.name: key_binding

description.summary: Define a key binding for the tabwin

event.description: Sent before done. Defines a keyboard shortcut for a tabwin action.

event.name: window

description.summary: A window entry for the tab switcher

event.description:
Creates a new tabwin window object. In phase one the compositor sends only static property events on it (no preview or icon); preview and icon data follow in phase two, after the UI process reports its sizes with image_sizes.
The compositor may also send this event after the build, if a new window appears while the tab switcher is active. The UI process should append it to the display.

event.name: done

description.summary: A batch of tabwin updates is complete

event.description:
Marks the tabwin state sent so far as consistent; the UI process should apply it. This event recurs after every batch rather than being sent once.
Phase one ends with this event, after every window's static properties have been sent; the UI process should then compute its layout and send image_sizes. Phase two ends with this event again, after preview and icon data have been sent for every window. Later live updates (a window added or removed) likewise end with it.

event.name: close

description.summary: Tells the UI process to close the tabwin

event.description: The compositor has dismissed the tabwin, and the UI process should clean up its resources.

interface.name: xfwl4_ui_tabwin_window_v1

description.summary: A window entry in the tab switcher

interface.description: Represents a single window in the tab switcher display. Created by the compositor via xfwl4_ui_tabwin_v1.window. The compositor sends property events to describe the window.

event.name: window_id
event.description: Used to correlate with initial_selection, hover, finished, and window_removed.

event.name: app_name
event.description: Optional

event.name: preview

description.summary: RGBA preview image of the window

event.description: Sent in phase two, after the UI process reports sizes with image_sizes, rendered to the requested preview size and scale. Optional; if not sent, the tabwin should fall back to the application icon.

event.name: app_icon_named
event.description: Sent in phase two if the compositor chose a themed icon name. At most one of the app_icon events is sent per window (the compositor's chosen icon); if none is sent, the tabwin should show a default fallback icon.

event.name: app_icon_file

description.summary: Application icon from a file path

event.description: Sent in phase two if the compositor chose a file on disk. At most one of the app_icon events is sent per window; if none is sent, the tabwin should show a default fallback icon.

event.name: app_icon_pixels

description.summary: Application icon as RGBA pixel data

event.description: Sent in phase two if the compositor chose raster pixel data, at the chosen size and scale. At most one of the app_icon events is sent per window; if none is sent, the tabwin should show a default fallback icon.

event.name: done

description.summary: A batch of this window's properties is complete

event.description: Marks the properties sent for this window entry as consistent; the UI process should apply them. This event recurs: once in phase one after the static properties, again in phase two after the preview and icon, and again for any later live update.

event.name: removed

description.summary: The window was removed during tab switching

event.description: The window disappeared while the tab switcher was active. The UI process may remove it from the tab switcher display. The window object is now destroyed, and no further requests should be sent to it.

interface.name: xfwl4_ui_window_menu_v1
interface.description: Represents an active window context menu. Created by the compositor via xfwl4_ui_manager_v1.create_window_menu. The compositor sends workspace/monitor data; the UI process sends user selections.

request.name: ready

description.summary: Window menu is ready to be shown

request.description: The UI process has created the window menu and is ready for the compositor to position the anchor surface.

request.name: action

description.summary: User selected a window menu action

request.description: The user selected an action from the window menu.

request.name: move_to_workspace
request.description: The user selected to move the window to a different workspace.

request.name: move_to_output
request.description: The user selected to move the window to an adjacent monitor.

request.name: dismissed
request.description:
The window menu was closed. Must be sent after one of the action requests above, or if the menu was dismissed without selecting anything.
This destroys the window menu object.

description.summary: The window this menu is for

event.name: is_maximized
event.description: If not sent, maximizing is not available for this window.

event.name: is_shaded
event.description: If not sent, shading is not available for this window.

event.name: is_fullscreen
event.description: If not sent, fullscreening is not available for this window.

event.name: workspace

description.summary: Describes a workspace for the window menu

event.description: Sent before done.

event.name: adjacent_monitor

description.summary: An adjacent monitor the window can move to

event.description: Sent before done. At most four of these events are sent (one per direction). The compositor computes monitor adjacency; the UI process just presents the options.

event.name: done

description.summary: All window menu state has been sent

event.description: Marks the end of state, workspace, and monitor events. The UI process should now create the menu and send ready when it is prepared.
